summary: Added the missing `typecheck` npm script (`tsc --noEmit`) so CLAUDE.md's documented `npm run typecheck` verification command now exists (previously only `npx tsc --noEmit` worked, and several docs noted 'no script'). Audited the release env and iOS config rather than blindly rewriting them: `.env.example` already documents the full 16-var EXPO_PUBLIC surface with safe beta defaults (Pro flags off, RevenueCat keys empty, entitlement/offering on code defaults) and is pinned by the §RE/§SL smoke checks, so no change was needed — and it stays read/write-denied so the sanctioned rm+Write path was intentionally not exercised since the file is already correct. iOS permission strings were verified end-to-end: the only permission-gated native capability is expo-speech-recognition, and both NSMicrophoneUsageDescription and NSSpeechRecognitionUsageDescription are present with descriptive, review-safe copy in both app.json (plugin config) and the prebuilt ios/Lullaby/Info.plist, with matching strings. No camera/photos/location/notifications/audio-recording APIs are used, so no further plist keys are required; ATS keeps NSAllowsArbitraryLoads=false. No EAS submit, no credential changes, no bundle-identifier change.
validation_notes: `npm run typecheck` (new script) -> tsc --noEmit exits 0. `npm run check:local-interactions` -> All 388 checks passed (includes §RE1-RE6 env-hygiene and §SL link/env checks that guard .env.example). Confirmed the smoke suite pins only existing scripts (android/dev/dev:clear) and never asserts a typecheck script is absent, so adding it is safe. Verified ios/Lullaby/Info.plist has exactly 2 UsageDescription keys (mic + speech) matching app.json's expo-speech-recognition plugin strings; grep found no other permission-gated native APIs in src/.
manual_followups: EAS Submit is unconfigured: eas.json `submit.production` is an empty object. Before the first App Store submission, a human must add App Store Connect credentials (ascAppId / appleId / appleTeamId or an App Store Connect API key) — do NOT commit secrets; use EAS secrets. Not done here per task rules (no eas submit, no credential changes).

Create the App Store Connect app record for bundle id com.lullaby.app (do not change the bundle identifier) and prepare the app privacy questionnaire: declare microphone + speech recognition usage; the app is local-only v1 with no data collection/tracking.

Host the real Privacy Policy and Terms of Use pages and set EXPO_PUBLIC_PRIVACY_POLICY_URL / EXPO_PUBLIC_TERMS_URL / EXPO_PUBLIC_SUPPORT_EMAIL in the production EAS env (they currently fall back to lullaby.app placeholders via src/lib/appLinks.ts).

Configure production EXPO_PUBLIC env values in the EAS `production` build profile / EAS secrets (Supabase URL+anon key if sync is enabled, Google web client id if used). .env.example ships all flags OFF and RevenueCat keys empty by design — production Pro requires the separate RevenueCat setup task (real keys + matching dashboard entitlement/offering ids + store products).

Server secrets (ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY, REASSURE_MODEL, REASSURE_NIGHT_READ_ENABLED) belong to Supabase edge-function config, never to the client bundle or .env.example — set them in the Supabase project, not in EXPO_PUBLIC vars.
apple_review_risk_remaining: Privacy Policy / Terms links resolve to lullaby.app placeholder URLs until real pages are hosted and the EXPO_PUBLIC_* URL vars are set for the production build; App Review may reject if those pages are not live and truthful.

App Store Connect app-privacy disclosure must accurately declare microphone + speech-recognition usage (hands-free question flow); this is a human data-safety questionnaire step, not enforceable in code.
EAS Submit remains unconfigured by design (task forbids eas submit / credential changes); shipping still requires the manual App Store Connect + EAS credential setup above.
